items.headline: City updates youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: San José presented status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. The city also shared progress on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, which will affect library services and physical spaces across the city.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled with no new date set yet. Residents interested in this development should watch for a re-noticed hearing announcement.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with new consultant agreement
items.summary: The city approved a third amendment to its consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, indicating ongoing work to improve safety at this busy intersection. The city also awarded a contract for a 2026 Local Streets pavement removal and replacement project.

items.headline: Mexican Heritage Plaza receives state arts and culture grant
items.summary: San José's School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza received a California Natural Resources Agency grant, supporting cultural programming and facility improvements at this community institution.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council reviewed Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, which includes revised criteria for evaluating bike lanes, pedestrian paths, and transit improvements. This plan will shape how Cupertino prioritizes investments in walking and cycling infrastructure over the coming years.

items.headline: City Council votes to change vacancy filling process
items.summary: The Council introduced an ordinance to amend how the city fills vacant Council seats. This change could affect how quickly the city can replace departing council members and may shift decision-making power between the Council and residents.

items.headline: 65-unit senior apartment complex approved on East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a nearly $9M senior housing development with 65 units at 678 E St John St. This addresses San José's growing need for dedicated senior housing amid an aging population.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition sues city over Mary Avenue safety
items.summary: The City is defending against litigation filed by the Garden Gate Coalition challenging Cupertino's handling of Mary Avenue safety issues. This case suggests ongoing community concerns about traffic or pedestrian safety on that street.
